title: Ladder Table
summary: A small two-tier leaning side table with angled legs, built from a couple of 1x3s and a 1x2. Easy cuts, but the leg angles make it a little tricky.
description:
This is a compact two-shelf side table with a ladder-like lean: the front legs rake forward and the back legs rake back, so the whole piece splays for a light, modern footprint. As the source title admits, it's 'super easy but a little tricky' — the joinery is all butt joints and screws, but the two different leg angles are what you have to get right.

The legs carry two angle cuts each, ends parallel: the back legs at 5° off square, the front legs at a steeper 20°. Parallel-end cuts matter — both ends of a leg tilt the same way, so the leg leans without twisting. Cut them carefully with a miter saw and keep the pairs matched.

The shelves are built as little sub-assemblies first: a frame of 1x2 supports, then 1x3 shelf boards screwed down onto the supports from underneath. Build the top and bottom shelves separately, then attach them to the angled legs. Because everything leans, dry-fit and check with a level before you commit the last screws — it's easy for a splayed piece to end up sitting cockeyed if one leg angle is off. Fill, sand, and finish. It's small and cheap enough to be a good practice piece for angled work.

tools.note: The angled parallel-end leg cuts are the whole trick — a miter saw makes them repeatable.
Check the splayed assembly sits true before the final screws.

cutList.note: Ends cut at 5° off square, ends parallel.
Ends cut at 20° off square, ends parallel.

steps.title: Build the top shelf supports
steps.body: Build the top shelf support frame from the 10-1/2" and 5" 1x2 pieces, screwing them together with 2" screws and glue. This is the little frame the top shelf boards will land on.

steps.title: Add the top shelf boards
steps.body: Fasten the three 14" 1x3 shelf boards down onto the support frame, screwing up through the supports into the underside of the boards with 2" screws and glue. Driving from below keeps the shelf top clean of screw heads.

steps.title: Build the bottom shelf
steps.body: Assemble the bottom shelf support frame the same way, from the 10-1/2" and 9" 1x2 pieces, with glue and 2" screws. The bottom shelf is a touch different in proportion because the legs are wider apart at the base.

steps.title: Add the bottom shelf boards
steps.body: Fasten the five 10-1/2" 1x3 boards down onto the bottom support frame, again screwing up through the supports into the underside of the boards with 2" screws and glue.

steps.title: Cut the angled legs and assemble
steps.body:
Cut the back legs with 5° parallel-end angles and the front legs with 20° parallel-end angles — parallel ends are what let each leg lean without twisting. Attach the shelves to the legs with 1-1/4" screws and glue.

Because the whole table splays, dry-fit and check it with a level before the last screws go in; a single off leg angle will leave it sitting cockeyed. Fill holes, sand, and finish.

images.alt: Small leaning ladder-style side table with two shelves and angled legs
